metadata.notes: Template for retrieval benchmarks. Duplicate this file, update metadata, expand queries to the desired benchmark size, then fill gold anchors after grounding/reanchor.

metadata_fields.batch_ids: string[] (batch identifiers merged into this benchmark; often ['001'] for a fresh benchmark)
metadata_fields.merged_at: string (ISO-8601 timestamp for template instantiation or merge time)
metadata_fields.current_run_id: string|null (latest retrieval/eval run id if available)
metadata_fields.schema: object (this schema description; optional in non-template benchmarks)

query_fields.id: string (stable id; do not change once published)
query_fields.question: string (the user-facing retrieval question)
query_fields.expected_answer_summary: string (short target answer summary; can be empty initially)
query_fields.source_page: number|string|null (authoritative source page if known; null if unknown)
query_fields.notes: string (what this is testing, ambiguity, cross-chapter requirements, etc.)
query_fields.legacy_gold_chunk_ids: string[] (optional legacy chunk ids from older corpora/benchmarks)
query_fields.gold_unit_ids: string[] (merged union of required_gold + supporting_gold; populated by grounding)
query_fields.required_gold: string[] (unit ids that MUST be retrieved for a correct answer)
query_fields.supporting_gold: string[] (unit ids that help but aren't strictly required)
query_fields._required_gold: string[] (internal normalized copy used by metrics)
query_fields._supporting_gold: string[] (internal normalized copy used by metrics)
query_fields.gold_locations: object (unit_id -> {page, structural_path, source_unit_ids}; populated by grounding)
query_fields.required_gold_rationale: object (unit_id -> string explaining why that unit is required)

queries.question: <FILL_ME: cross-section or synthesis question>
queries.notes: <FILL_ME: why this is hard or what retrieval behavior it should exercise>

queries.question: <FILL_ME: higher-difficulty reasoning or edge-case question>
queries.notes: <FILL_ME: edge case, ambiguity, or multi-hop intent>
